title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Pub/Sub and Reliable Messaging with TypeScript 7

audience.description: TypeScript backend developers who can build production HTTP services and need to design, implement, test, operate, and defend asynchronous RabbitMQ systems.
entryKnowledge: Build and defend a production Node 24 and TypeScript HTTP service with validation, persistence, lifecycle, security, observability, and recovery.
Explain transactions, idempotency, concurrency, timeouts, backpressure, and failure ownership across HTTP and PostgreSQL boundaries.
deviceConstraints: Modern browser; learner TypeScript runs only in the isolated deterministic compiler runtime. Browser tasks model topology, routing, delivery, acknowledgement, retry, and recovery decisions without connecting to RabbitMQ, opening sockets, reading host state, using credentials, or executing external effects.
accessibilityAssumptions: Topology diagrams, message timelines, delivery state machines, routing tables, traces, and dashboards include equivalent structured text, keyboard operation, announced status, and non-color meaning.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Build and defend a production Node 24 and TypeScript HTTP service with validation, persistence, lifecycle, security, observability, and recovery.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Explain transactions, idempotency, concurrency, timeouts, backpressure, and failure ownership across HTTP and PostgreSQL boundaries.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 5 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: RabbitMQ 4.3.2, AMQP 0-9-1, Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, architecture selection, exchanges, queues, bindings, connections, channels, heartbeats, recovery, topology, direct and fanout pub/sub, topic and headers routing, envelopes, AsyncAPI 3.1, CloudEvents 1.0.2, publisher backpressure, returns, confirms, consumers, acknowledgements, prefetch, idempotency, retries, dead lettering, quorum queues, RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry and priorities, ordering, streams, outbox and sagas, TLS, authorization, OpenTelemetry 1.43, Prometheus, capacity, testing, upgrades, and recovery
Runnable deterministic pure-TypeScript messaging decision functions plus explicit clean compilation and disposable RabbitMQ, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates
Five cumulative messaging systems and a performance-based production defense exam
excludes: Browser access to RabbitMQ, AMQP sockets, Docker, host processes, credentials, external databases, third-party effects, management APIs, or production infrastructure
Claiming exact broker, replication, TLS, partition, throughput, or recovery behavior from pure browser models without the named authorized live transfer gate

sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves TypeScript, HTTP service, accessibility, security, validation, persistence, testing, lifecycle, and evidence habits before adding one messaging boundary.

Browser TypeScript is deterministic and isolated. It models topology, routing, delivery, acknowledgement, retry, and recovery decisions but never imports amqplib or Node built-ins, opens sockets, contacts RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, management APIs, databases, or external effects, reads host state, or uses credentials.

Passing work requires stable message and scenario identity, a named ownership transfer, bounded bytes, time, attempts, and concurrency, observable changed and failure results, cleanup and recovery, and explicit disposable-broker or production transfer limits.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.

competencies.statement: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.
misconceptions: Consumer tags remain valid after a channel is recreated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.

competencies.statement: Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.
misconceptions: The delivery callback always receives a message object until the application calls cancel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.

competencies.statement: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel.
misconceptions: basic.cancel automatically requeues all deliveries already dispatched to the consumer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel.

competencies.statement: Choose acknowledgement, requeue, dead-letter, or channel-close behavior for in-flight work during graceful and forced shutdown.
misconceptions: Acknowledging every in-flight message during shutdown prevents both loss and duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose acknowledgement, requeue, dead-letter, or channel-close behavior for in-flight work during graceful and forced shutdown.

competencies.statement: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
misconceptions: Automatic acknowledgement means RabbitMQ waits for handler success before deleting the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.

competencies.statement: Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.
misconceptions: Delivery tags are globally unique across a connection and survive channel recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.

competencies.statement: Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.
misconceptions: Acknowledging before a database commit improves safety because it shortens the unacked window.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.

competencies.statement: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.
misconceptions: redelivered false proves no consumer has ever seen the message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.

competencies.statement: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
misconceptions: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.

competencies.statement: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.
misconceptions: Prefetch controls the total number of ready messages stored in the queue.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.

competencies.statement: Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.
misconceptions: Setting prefetch alone limits every asynchronous task a handler may start.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.

competencies.statement: Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
misconceptions: Consumer capacity below 100 percent always means adding consumers will fix throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.

competencies.statement: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.
misconceptions: Prefetch can be continuously maximized from one recent latency sample without instability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.

competencies.statement: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.
misconceptions: One shared FIFO queue guarantees fair service across tenants and task costs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.

competencies.statement: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ delivery tags are suitable permanent idempotency keys.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.

competencies.statement: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
misconceptions: Checking then inserting an inbox row in separate transactions prevents concurrent duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.

competencies.statement: Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
misconceptions: Any later message with the same ID should return the first result even if its payload changed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.

competencies.statement: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.
misconceptions: A local inbox transaction makes an external API call exactly once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.

competencies.statement: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.
misconceptions: Inbox records can be deleted immediately after acknowledgement because RabbitMQ will not redeliver.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.

competencies.statement: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
misconceptions: Exception class alone reliably determines whether a message should be retried.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.

competencies.statement: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
misconceptions: A maximum attempt count alone prevents stale work and retry storms.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.

competencies.statement: Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.
misconceptions: Sleeping inside each consumer handler is an efficient delayed retry mechanism.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.

competencies.statement: Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message.
misconceptions: Returning every message with delay is always safer than pausing consumers during a total outage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message.

competencies.statement: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.
misconceptions: Queue depth alone explains whether retries are healthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.

competencies.statement: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
misconceptions: Deleting or expiring an entire queue dead-letters all of its messages.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.

competencies.statement: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ creates a missing dead-letter exchange automatically when the first message fails.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.

competencies.statement: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ always detects every dead-letter cycle and reports it to publishers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.

competencies.statement: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.
misconceptions: Quorum queues retry poison messages forever unless an application counts attempts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.

competencies.statement: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.
misconceptions: Moving a message to a dead-letter queue completes incident handling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.

competencies.statement: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
misconceptions: Quorum queues are the best default for every queue regardless of lifetime or workload.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.

competencies.statement: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
misconceptions: A three-member quorum queue remains available after any two members fail.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.

competencies.statement: Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
misconceptions: Declaring x-queue-type quorum alone guarantees no loss or duplicates end to end.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.

competencies.statement: Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.
misconceptions: Clients must connect to the leader node or RabbitMQ rejects queue operations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints.

competencies.statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.
misconceptions: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

competencies.statement: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
misconceptions: Delayed retry is enabled by default for every quorum queue in RabbitMQ 4.3.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.

competencies.statement: Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
misconceptions: TTL dead-letter cycles and native delayed retry have identical safety and resource behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.

competencies.statement: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
misconceptions: Strict priority guarantees low-priority messages eventually run under continuous high-priority load.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.

competencies.statement: Align broker consumer timeout, handler deadline, dependency timeout, cancellation, redelivery, and channel recovery without duplicate terminal decisions.
misconceptions: A consumer timeout cleanly cancels application work and proves no external effect completed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align broker consumer timeout, handler deadline, dependency timeout, cancellation, redelivery, and channel recovery without duplicate terminal decisions.

competencies.statement: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.
misconceptions: Compiling against amqplib 2.0.1 guarantees the connected broker supports RabbitMQ 4.3 features.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

competencies.statement: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ preserves one global order across all publishers, queues, and consumer completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers.

competencies.statement: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.
misconceptions: Hashing a key across queues preserves order when partition count changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.

competencies.statement: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.
misconceptions: An exclusive consumer and single active consumer provide identical failover behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits.

competencies.statement: Defend state with entity version, sequence, monotonic transition, deduplication, gap detection, and reconciliation rather than transport order alone.
misconceptions: Discarding any message older than the latest timestamp safely resolves all ordering races.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend state with entity version, sequence, monotonic transition, deduplication, gap detection, and reconciliation rather than transport order alone.

competencies.statement: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope.
misconceptions: Strict global order can scale horizontally without coordination cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope.

competencies.statement: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
misconceptions: Streams are simply queues that keep acknowledged messages a little longer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.

competencies.statement: Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
misconceptions: A stream retains all messages forever unless every consumer acknowledges them.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.

competencies.statement: Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
misconceptions: A super stream provides global ordering across all partitions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.

competencies.statement: Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.
misconceptions: Stream clients can use any cluster node with the same transparent routing as queues.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.

competencies.statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
misconceptions: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.

competencies.statement: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
misconceptions: Publishing immediately after a database commit makes the two operations atomic enough.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.

competencies.statement: Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
misconceptions: An outbox row written in a separate transaction closes the dual-write gap.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.

competencies.statement: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
misconceptions: The relay can delete a row as soon as publish returns true.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.

competencies.statement: Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.
misconceptions: Inbox plus outbox provides exactly-once transport and removes all duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.

competencies.statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
misconceptions: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
misconceptions: Encrypting AMQP without verifying the broker certificate prevents man-in-the-middle attacks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.

competencies.statement: Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ user tags grant applications exchange and queue permissions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.

competencies.statement: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.
misconceptions: Read permission allows publishing and write permission allows consuming.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.

competencies.statement: Load credentials from approved secret delivery, redact URLs and errors, update or reconnect under bounded rotation, and prove old access revocation.
misconceptions: Embedding credentials in an AMQP URL is safe because URL objects automatically redact passwords.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load credentials from approved secret delivery, redact URLs and errors, update or reconnect under bounded rotation, and prove old access revocation.

competencies.statement: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.
misconceptions: A tenant header is sufficient authorization for routing and consuming tenant data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.

competencies.statement: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
misconceptions: One end-to-end span can stay open for every message until all subscribers finish.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.

competencies.statement: Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.
misconceptions: A queue depth of zero proves consumers are healthy and caught up.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.

competencies.statement: Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.
misconceptions: Average message rate alone determines required consumer count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.

competencies.statement: Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.
misconceptions: Increasing prefetch or publisher concurrency clears a broker memory alarm faster.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.

competencies.statement: Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts.
misconceptions: Broker uptime is an adequate end-to-end messaging SLO.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts.

competencies.statement: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.
misconceptions: Mocking channel.publish and channel.consume proves RabbitMQ integration behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.

competencies.statement: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
misconceptions: Killing one broker process is sufficient chaos coverage for every messaging failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.

competencies.statement: Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
misconceptions: Deploying producers before consumers is always backward compatible when JSON is used.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.

competencies.statement: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits.
misconceptions: RabbitMQ 3.13 clusters can roll directly to 4.3 one node at a time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits.

competencies.statement: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.
misconceptions: Passing unit tests and a local Docker demo proves production messaging readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.

modules.title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
